valmiera-cesis.description: A full day on the upper Gauja through open meadows and into dense forest. The river widens and narrows through varied terrain — great introduction to multi-hour paddling.

highlights.name: Open meadow sections
highlights.desc: The upper Gauja flows through wide open farmland — great visibility, gentle breeze, and a relaxed pace to warm up.

highlights.name: Forest entry
highlights.desc: Around the halfway point the landscape closes in as the river enters dense mixed forest — the atmosphere completely changes.

highlights.name: Gentle current
highlights.desc: Consistently easy water throughout — ideal for groups of mixed experience or those doing their first longer paddle.

cesis-sigulda.description: The classic 2-day Gauja experience. Camp overnight by the river and paddle through the most scenic sections of Gauja National Park — sandstone cliffs, caves, and deep forest valleys.

highlights.name: Sandstone cliffs
highlights.desc: The most dramatic cliffs on the entire Gauja are found on this stretch — some reaching 20m above the waterline.

highlights.name: Riverside camping
highlights.desc: Spend a night on the riverbank surrounded by forest — one of the best camping experiences in Latvia.

highlights.name: Caves and overhangs
highlights.desc: Several accessible sandstone caves and rock shelters line this section — worth stopping to explore.

strenci-valmiera.description: A relaxed upper-Gauja paddle through open countryside. Perfect for beginners and families — wide river, gentle current, beautiful rural Latvian scenery.

highlights.name: Wide calm river
highlights.desc: The Gauja is at its widest here — easy to navigate and a great confidence-builder for first-time paddlers.

highlights.name: Rural Latvian scenery
highlights.desc: Farmhouses, meadows, and traditional Latvian countryside line the banks — peaceful and unhurried.

highlights.name: Valmiera finish
highlights.desc: End in the lively town of Valmiera with easy access to cafes, transport, and accommodation.

grivini-cesis.description: An exciting approach to Cēsis through increasingly dramatic scenery. The river picks up pace as the valley deepens — a great day trip with a rewarding finish.

highlights.name: Valley deepens
highlights.desc: The Gauja valley becomes progressively more dramatic as you approach Cēsis — banks rising higher with each bend.

highlights.name: Increasing pace
highlights.desc: The current gradually quickens in the lower section adding a satisfying sense of momentum.

highlights.name: Cēsis town
highlights.desc: Finish in one of Latvia's most charming historic towns — with a medieval castle, old town, and great restaurants.

cesis-ligatne.description: The most popular single-day trip on the Gauja. Paddle through the heart of the national park past Eagle Rock, sandstone caves, and stunning cliff faces. A classic for a reason.

highlights.name: Eagle Rock
highlights.desc: A spectacular 25m sandstone cliff rising sheer from the water — one of the most photographed spots on any Latvian river.

highlights.name: Sandstone caves
highlights.desc: Several caves are accessible directly from the river — great for a brief stop and exploration.

highlights.name: National Park core
highlights.desc: This section passes through the most protected and pristine part of Gauja National Park — no roads, no houses, just nature.

ligatne-sigulda.description: Continue downstream through the deepest, most dramatic section of the Gauja Valley. Pass under the famous Līgatne cable car and arrive near Turaida Castle.

highlights.name: Līgatne cable car
highlights.desc: Pass directly beneath this historic ferry-style cable car — one of the few still operating in Latvia.

highlights.name: Turaida Castle views
highlights.desc: As you approach Sigulda, the towers of Turaida Castle become visible above the treeline — a stunning sight from the water.

highlights.name: Deep valley
highlights.desc: The valley walls reach their highest point on this stretch — sheer cliffs and forested slopes on both sides.

sigulda-ramkalni.description: A shorter section below Sigulda with calmer water and beautiful forest scenery. Good option for a relaxed afternoon paddle or for less experienced groups.

highlights.name: Calm water
highlights.desc: The river mellows out below Sigulda — ideal for a relaxed paddle without the pressure of technical sections.

highlights.name: Forest scenery
highlights.desc: Dense mixed forest lines both banks throughout — quiet, shaded, and beautiful in any season.

highlights.name: Half-day option
highlights.desc: Short enough for an afternoon trip — great for groups with limited time or those new to paddling.

ligatne-ramkalni.description: A 2-day route covering the lower Gauja valley with camping overnight. Takes in the best of the lower sections in a relaxed multi-day format.

highlights.name: Riverside camping
highlights.desc: Camp by the river under a forest canopy — one of the most atmospheric overnight spots on the lower Gauja.

highlights.name: Lower Gauja
highlights.desc: The river widens and slows in the lower valley — long, peaceful stretches with wildlife and birdsong.

highlights.name: Multi-day rhythm
highlights.desc: Two days gives you time to stop, swim, explore, and truly settle into the pace of river life.

valmiera-sigulda.description: The full Gauja traverse — 3 days from Valmiera all the way to Sigulda. Camp two nights riverside and experience every mood of Latvia's most famous river.

highlights.name: Full river traverse
highlights.desc: Experience the entire character of the Gauja — from open farmland to dramatic forested gorge over three days.

highlights.name: Two nights camping
highlights.desc: Two separate riverside campsites let you fully immerse in the river environment — no rushing.

highlights.name: Every landscape
highlights.desc: Open meadows, dense forest, sandstone cliffs, caves, and the Sigulda valley — all in one epic journey.

valmiera-ligatne.description: Three days on the upper and middle Gauja — the perfect balance of distance and scenery without the crowds near Sigulda.

highlights.name: Upper and middle Gauja
highlights.desc: The best of both worlds — open countryside upstream and increasingly dramatic valley scenery downstream.

highlights.name: Less crowded
highlights.desc: This section sees far fewer paddlers than the Cēsis–Sigulda stretch — more space, more wildlife, more peace.

highlights.name: Wild camping
highlights.desc: Three days allows for two relaxed camping nights in some of the quietest riverside spots on the river.

janramis-sigulda.description: A 3-day journey ending at Sigulda — starts from a quiet forest launch point and builds through increasingly scenic terrain to the dramatic Gauja Valley finale.

highlights.name: Forest start
highlights.desc: Launch from a quiet forest clearing — no crowds, no noise, just the sound of the river.

highlights.name: Builds in drama
highlights.desc: Each day gets more scenic as the valley deepens and the cliffs grow taller.

highlights.name: Sigulda finale
highlights.desc: Arrive at Sigulda — the most dramatic finish on the Gauja, with castle views and a lively town waiting.

melturi-veclaucu-tilts.description: A full day on the Amata river through deep forest gorges and past dramatic rock formations. The Amata is narrower and more intimate than the Gauja — a true hidden gem.

highlights.name: Deep forest gorge
highlights.desc: The Amata cuts through a steep-sided gorge completely enclosed by old-growth forest — unlike anything on the Gauja.

highlights.name: Rock formations
highlights.desc: Sandstone outcrops and overhanging walls appear around almost every bend — bring a camera.

highlights.name: Intimate river
highlights.desc: The Amata is narrow enough that you can touch both banks — a completely different feel to the wider rivers.

zvartes-iezis-veclaucu-tilts.description: Start your paddle directly at the foot of Zvārtes Rock — one of Latvia's most spectacular natural landmarks. The river winds through forested valleys to a calm finish.

highlights.name: Zvārtes Rock
highlights.desc: Start directly beneath this 15m sandstone monolith — one of the most dramatic launch points on any Latvian river.

highlights.name: Forested valley
highlights.desc: The entire route flows through pristine forest with no roads or buildings visible from the water.

highlights.name: Calm finish
highlights.desc: The river mellows beautifully in the lower section — a relaxed end to a dramatic beginning.

mazsalaca-licu-skola.description: A gentle intro to the Salaca — clear water, sandy banks, and forest scenery. Perfect for families and first-time paddlers wanting to experience this beautiful river.

highlights.name: Crystal-clear water
highlights.desc: The Salaca's famously transparent water lets you watch fish and the sandy riverbed the entire way.

highlights.name: Sandy banks
highlights.desc: Natural sandy beaches appear along the route — perfect for swimming stops and picnic breaks.

highlights.name: Family friendly
highlights.desc: Gentle current and short distance make this ideal for children and first-time paddlers.

mazsalaca-staicele.description: Two days on the most scenic stretch of the Salaca. Red sandstone outcrops rise above crystal-clear water — camp overnight and experience the river at its most wild.

highlights.name: Red sandstone cliffs
highlights.desc: The Salaca's signature red-orange sandstone formations are at their most dramatic on this stretch.

highlights.name: Wild camping
highlights.desc: Camp overnight on the river — fall asleep to the sound of the current and wake up surrounded by forest.

highlights.name: Crystal-clear water
highlights.desc: The Salaca's extraordinary water clarity makes this stretch feel like paddling through a natural aquarium.

staicele-salacgriva.description: The lower Salaca to the sea — the river widens as it approaches Salacgrīva and the Baltic. A unique experience paddling from forest into open coastal landscape.

highlights.name: River meets the sea
highlights.desc: A rare experience — paddle from forest river all the way to the Baltic coast in a single journey.

highlights.name: Coastal landscape
highlights.desc: The final kilometres open into a completely different environment — reed beds, open sky, and sea breeze.

highlights.name: Salacgrīva town
highlights.desc: Finish in a genuine Latvian coastal fishing town with fresh fish, harbour views, and local character.

staicele-sarkanas-klintis.description: A day trip to the famous Red Cliffs — dramatic sandstone formations rising directly from the water. One of the most photogenic spots on any Latvian river.

highlights.name: Red Cliffs
highlights.desc: The Sarkanas Klintis are the Salaca's signature landmark — vivid red-orange sandstone walls rising 10m from the water.

highlights.name: Most photogenic
highlights.desc: This is the most photographed stretch of any Latvian river — dramatic colour contrast in every direction.

highlights.name: Accessible day trip
highlights.desc: Short enough to complete comfortably in a day with plenty of time to stop and enjoy the scenery.

mazsalaca-viku-tilts.description: A 2-day Salaca route through varied terrain — open meadows give way to forested banks and sandstone gorges. A well-rounded introduction to multi-day paddling.

highlights.name: Varied terrain
highlights.desc: The landscape shifts from open countryside to forested banks to sandstone gorge — no two sections feel the same.

highlights.name: Sandstone gorges
highlights.desc: The middle section enters a narrow gorge with red sandstone walls on both sides — the highlight of the route.

highlights.name: Multi-day intro
highlights.desc: Two days at a relaxed pace — ideal for those trying their first overnight paddling trip.

rozula-vejini.description: A short, exciting run on the Brasla with fast water and forest scenery. Perfect for those wanting a taste of moving water without committing to a full day.

highlights.name: Fast water
highlights.desc: The Brasla moves quickly here — a proper current that keeps you engaged and moving throughout.

highlights.name: Dense forest
highlights.desc: Both banks are wall-to-wall forest — completely enclosed and wild-feeling despite the short distance.

highlights.name: Half-day option
highlights.desc: Short enough for a morning or afternoon — perfect if you want the Brasla experience without a full-day commitment.

rozula-a3-tilts.description: A full day on the Brasla from the classic Rozula launch point. Fast sections alternate with calmer pools — great variety and beautiful gorge scenery throughout.

highlights.name: Classic launch point
highlights.desc: Rozula is the traditional starting point for Brasla paddling — well-organised, easy access, familiar to guides.

highlights.name: Fast and calm variety
highlights.desc: The route alternates between exciting fast sections and calm pools — keeps the day interesting throughout.

highlights.name: Gorge scenery
highlights.desc: The Brasla gorge is at its most dramatic here — steep forested banks and dramatic overhangs.

placis-a3-tilts.description: Launch from Plācis and paddle to the A3 bridge — a well-known Brasla day route with consistent moving water and great scenery.

highlights.name: Consistent water
highlights.desc: Good current throughout with no long flat sections — the Brasla keeps you moving and engaged the whole way.

highlights.name: Forested banks
highlights.desc: Tall trees and dense undergrowth line both banks — completely wild-feeling and beautifully shaded.

highlights.name: Well-marked route
highlights.desc: A popular and well-organised route — easy to follow with clear takeout points and good local knowledge.

vejini-a3-tilts.description: The lower section of the Brasla — slightly calmer than the upper reaches but still with good current and beautiful forest-lined banks.

highlights.name: Good current
highlights.desc: Still plenty of flow in the lower Brasla — enough to keep you moving without requiring constant paddling.

highlights.name: Forested banks
highlights.desc: The lower section is especially green and enclosed — tall forest on both sides from start to finish.

highlights.name: Relaxed pace
highlights.desc: Calmer than the upper reaches — good option for those wanting Brasla scenery without the more technical sections.

placis-ieteka-gauja.description: Paddle the Brasla all the way to its confluence with the Gauja — a satisfying full-day route that ends where two great rivers meet.

highlights.name: Full day on the Brasla
highlights.desc: Experience the complete lower Brasla from launch to confluence — the most complete one-day Brasla route.

highlights.name: River confluence
highlights.desc: Arrive at the exact point where the Brasla joins the Gauja — a dramatic and memorable finish.

highlights.name: Two rivers in one
highlights.desc: Briefly join the Gauja at the end for a different perspective on Latvia's most famous river.

vejini-ieteka-gauja.description: From Vējiņi to the Gauja confluence — a great option for those who want the Brasla experience with a shorter upper section.

highlights.name: Gauja confluence
highlights.desc: The Brasla meets the Gauja in a beautiful wide stretch — a natural and satisfying place to finish.

highlights.name: Good current
highlights.desc: Solid flow from start to finish — the Brasla doesn't disappoint even in its lower reaches.

highlights.name: Shorter than Rozula
highlights.desc: A slightly less demanding option that still delivers the full Brasla character and scenery.

straupe-a3-tilts.description: Launch from Straupe and follow the Brasla downstream to the A3 bridge — a classic route through one of the most scenic parts of the river.

highlights.name: Straupe village
highlights.desc: Start your paddle in the picturesque village of Straupe — worth arriving early to explore before launching.

highlights.name: Classic Brasla scenery
highlights.desc: This section showcases everything the Brasla does best — fast water, forest, and gorge walls.

highlights.name: Easy access
highlights.desc: Good road access at both launch and takeout makes logistics simple for groups.

straupe-ieteka-gauja.description: A longer day on the lower Brasla from Straupe all the way to the Gauja. Takes in the best of the lower river with a memorable confluence finish.

highlights.name: Straupe to Gauja
highlights.desc: Cover the full lower Brasla in one day — a proper adventure from historic village to river confluence.

highlights.name: Lower Brasla at its best
highlights.desc: The lower section combines all the Brasla's best features — current, scenery, and quiet forest.

highlights.name: Confluence finish
highlights.desc: End where the Brasla flows into the Gauja — one of the most satisfying finishes in Latvian paddling.

rozula-ieteka-gauja.description: Two full days on the Brasla from top to bottom. Camp overnight mid-river and experience the full character of this exciting tributary — fast, scenic, and wild.

highlights.name: Full Brasla traverse
highlights.desc: The only route that covers the entire Brasla from Rozula to the Gauja — the definitive Brasla experience.

highlights.name: Overnight camp
highlights.desc: Camp mid-river surrounded by forest — the Brasla is at its most magical in the evening light.

highlights.name: Fast and wild
highlights.desc: The Brasla's character — quick current, forest gorge, dramatic banks — experienced over two full days.

placis-sigulda.description: A 2-day adventure combining the Brasla with the Gauja — paddle down the Brasla to the confluence then continue on the Gauja to Sigulda. Two rivers in one trip.

highlights.name: Two rivers
highlights.desc: Experience the fast, intimate Brasla and then the wide, dramatic Gauja — two completely different paddling environments.

highlights.name: River confluence
highlights.desc: The moment the Brasla joins the Gauja is one of the most dramatic in Latvian paddling — the rivers are completely different in character.

highlights.name: Sigulda finish
highlights.desc: End in Sigulda — the castle, the valley views, and a well-earned meal in one of the town's restaurants.

straupe-sigulda.description: Start on the Brasla at Straupe, join the Gauja at the confluence, and finish at Sigulda. A spectacular 2-day route through two of Latvia's best rivers.

highlights.name: Brasla to Gauja
highlights.desc: Paddle from the intimate Brasla gorge into the wide open Gauja valley — a spectacular change of scale.

highlights.name: Spectacular scenery
highlights.desc: Two days of some of the best river scenery in Latvia — from Straupe's forest to Sigulda's dramatic valley.

highlights.name: Sigulda finish
highlights.desc: Arrive at Sigulda from the water — the castle towers visible from the river as you approach the finish.
